title: Ignition Failure
shortDescription: The unit failed to ignite after multiple attempts.
fullDescription:
Error E003 on Navien tankless water heaters indicates the unit attempted ignition but failed to detect a flame. The ignition sequence typically tries three times before locking out with this error code.

This is one of the most common Navien error codes and can be caused by gas supply issues, a dirty or failed igniter, or a problem with the flame detection rod. In many cases, the issue is related to insufficient gas pressure or a closed gas valve.

If the unit has been recently installed, verify that the gas line is properly sized for the BTU demand and that all gas connections are tight and leak-free.
safetyWarning: This error involves the gas ignition system. If you smell gas, evacuate immediately and call your gas company. Do not attempt to manually light the burner.

causes.cause: Gas supply valve closed or partially closed
Insufficient gas pressure to the unit
Air in the gas line after new installation

symptoms: Unit does not produce hot water
Unit locks out after failed attempts

fixSteps.title: Check Gas Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the gas shutoff valve is fully open and that other gas appliances in the home are working properly.

fixSteps.title: Reset the Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the unit off, wait 30 seconds, and turn it back on to clear the lockout and allow a fresh ignition attempt.

fixSteps.title: Clean the Igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front cover and gently clean the igniter electrode with fine sandpaper or emery cloth. Ensure the gap is approximately 3mm.

fixSteps.title: Clean the Flame Rod
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully clean the flame detection rod with fine steel wool or emery cloth to remove carbon buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check Gas Pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Use a manometer to measure gas pressure at the unit inlet. Compare to the specification on the rating plate.

whenToCallPro: If the gas supply is confirmed and igniter cleaning does not resolve the issue, call a licensed plumber or Navien-certified technician to check gas pressure and internal components.

faq.q: How many times does Navien try to ignite before showing E003?
faq.a: The unit typically attempts ignition three times before locking out with E003.

faq.q: Can air in the gas line cause E003?
faq.a: Yes, especially after a new installation or if the gas supply was interrupted. Purging the line usually resolves this.

faq.q: Is E003 dangerous?
faq.a: The unit safely shuts down when ignition fails. However, if you smell gas, evacuate and call your gas company immediately.

title: False Flame Detection
shortDescription: The flame rod detects a flame signal when the burner should be off.
fullDescription:
Error E004 indicates the flame detection rod is sensing a flame signal when the gas valve is closed and no flame should be present. This is a safety concern because it suggests either a short circuit in the flame detection wiring or a residual flame that has not fully extinguished.

The most common cause is a wiring issue with the flame rod circuit, but it can also be caused by a faulty control board that is misreading the flame signal. In rare cases, a gas valve that does not fully close can allow a small residual flame.

This error requires prompt attention as it relates to the gas safety system of the unit.
safetyWarning: This error may indicate a gas valve that is not closing properly. If you smell gas or see a flame when the unit should be off, shut off the gas supply immediately and call a professional.

causes.cause: Short circuit in flame rod wiring

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the unit off and unplug it for 60 seconds, then plug it back in and restart.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Flame Rod Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the flame rod wiring for damage, pinched insulation, or contact with the burner housing that could cause a short.

fixSteps.title: Clean or Replace Flame Rod
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the flame rod. If the ceramic insulator is cracked, replace the rod.

fixSteps.title: Test Flame Rod Circuit
fixSteps.instruction: With the unit off, measure the resistance between the flame rod terminal and ground. It should read open (infinite resistance).

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after cleaning the flame rod and checking wiring, a technician should test the control board and gas valve.

faq.q: What is a false flame?
faq.a: It means the unit detects a flame signal when no flame should exist, usually due to an electrical fault.

faq.q: Can I still use the water heater with E004?
faq.a: No. The unit will not operate with this error and should not be bypassed.

title: Abnormal Combustion / Air Pressure
shortDescription: The unit detected abnormal air pressure in the combustion chamber.
fullDescription:
Error E010 on Navien tankless water heaters indicates the air pressure sensor detected abnormal conditions in the combustion chamber or venting system. This could mean blocked or restricted venting, a failed combustion fan, or a problem with the air pressure sensor itself.

Proper venting is critical for safe operation. Navien units use sealed combustion with PVC or polypropylene venting. Any restriction, blockage, or improper termination can trigger this error.

If the unit is installed in a confined space, ensure adequate combustion air supply is available. Wind conditions at the vent termination can also intermittently cause this error.
safetyWarning: Combustion venting issues can lead to carbon monoxide buildup. Ensure CO detectors are working. Do not operate the unit if you suspect venting problems.

causes.cause: Bird nest or debris in vent termination
Excessive vent pipe length or elbows

fixSteps.title: Inspect Vent Termination
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and check the vent termination for blockages such as bird nests, leaves, ice, or snow.

fixSteps.title: Check Vent Pipe
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the entire vent run for disconnected joints, sags, or obstructions. Ensure all joints are properly cemented.

fixSteps.title: Verify Vent Length
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the total vent length and number of elbows do not exceed the specifications in the installation manual.

fixSteps.title: Check Combustion Fan
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the combustion fan running when the unit attempts to fire. If it is not running or sounds labored, it may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the vent is clear and properly installed but the error persists, call a Navien-certified technician to check the fan and pressure sensor.

faq.q: Can wind cause E010?
faq.a: Yes, strong winds at the vent termination can cause intermittent E010 errors. A wind-resistant vent cap may help.

faq.q: How long can the vent pipe be?
faq.a: Check your specific model's installation manual. Typical maximum is 60-100 feet equivalent with deductions for elbows.

title: Flame Loss During Operation
shortDescription: The flame was detected at ignition but lost during burner operation.
fullDescription:
Error E012 indicates the Navien unit successfully ignited but the flame was lost during operation. Unlike E003 (failure to ignite), E012 means the flame established but could not be maintained.

This typically points to intermittent gas supply issues, a dirty flame rod that loses signal as carbon builds up, or combustion air problems. The error can also occur if the gas line is undersized, causing gas pressure to drop when demand increases.

If this error occurs intermittently, it may be related to other gas appliances in the home drawing gas simultaneously, reducing supply pressure to the Navien unit.
safetyWarning: Flame loss can indicate gas supply instability. If you smell gas at any point, shut off the main gas supply and call your gas company.
symptoms: Hot water goes cold during use
Unit restarts after a brief pause

fixSteps.title: Clean the Flame Rod
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front cover and clean the flame detection rod with fine emery cloth or steel wool to remove carbon deposits.

fixSteps.title: Check Condensate Drain
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the condensate drain line is not clogged. Clear any blockages and verify water flows freely.

fixSteps.title: Verify Gas Pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Measure inlet gas pressure while the unit is firing at full capacity. Pressure should not drop below the minimum specified on the rating plate.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Venting
fixSteps.instruction: Check the vent system for any restrictions or disconnected joints that could cause combustion instability.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the flame rod does not resolve the issue, a technician should perform a full combustion analysis and gas pressure test.

faq.q: Why does my hot water go cold mid-shower?
faq.a: E012 causes the unit to shut down mid-operation. The flame rod is the most common culprit and should be cleaned first.

faq.q: How often should the flame rod be cleaned?
faq.a: Annually as part of routine maintenance, or sooner if E012 occurs.

title: Overheating - Abnormal Temperature Rise
shortDescription: The outlet water temperature has exceeded the safe limit.
fullDescription:
Error E015 indicates the Navien unit detected an abnormally high outlet water temperature exceeding approximately 185 degrees Fahrenheit. The unit shuts down to prevent scalding and component damage.

This can occur if the water flow rate is too low (scale buildup in the heat exchanger), the flow sensor is malfunctioning, or the gas valve is allowing too much gas. Hard water areas are particularly prone to scale buildup that restricts flow through the heat exchanger.

Repeatedly operating at excessively high temperatures can damage the heat exchanger, which is the most expensive component in the unit.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: Water above 150 degrees Fahrenheit causes severe scalding in seconds. Do not use hot water until this error is resolved. Allow the unit to cool before servicing.

causes.cause: Scale buildup in heat exchanger restricting flow
Malfunctioning flow sensor reading too high
Partially closed water valve reducing flow

fixSteps.title: Check Water Valves
fixSteps.instruction: Verify both the cold water inlet and hot water outlet valves are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Clean Inlet Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the cold water inlet filter screen. Sediment buildup can restrict flow.

fixSteps.title: Flush the Heat Exchanger
fixSteps.instruction: Connect a descaling pump and circulate a vinegar or commercial descaling solution through the heat exchanger for 45-60 minutes using the isolation valves.

fixSteps.title: Check Flow Rate
fixSteps.instruction: After flushing, measure the hot water flow rate. It should meet the minimum activation rate listed in your manual (typically 0.5 GPM).

whenToCallPro: If descaling does not restore proper flow and the error continues, the heat exchanger may be severely scaled or the flow sensor may need replacement.

faq.q: How do I prevent scale buildup?
faq.a: Install a water softener or whole-house scale prevention system, and flush the heat exchanger annually with descaling solution.

faq.q: What descaling solution should I use?
faq.a: White vinegar is effective for light scale. For heavy buildup, use a commercial tankless water heater descaling solution.

title: Overheating - Heat Exchanger
shortDescription: The heat exchanger temperature has exceeded the maximum safe operating limit.
fullDescription:
Error E016 signals that the heat exchanger itself has overheated, which is distinct from E015 (outlet water temperature). The heat exchanger temperature sensor detected a reading above the safe threshold, typically around 203 degrees Fahrenheit.

This error often indicates severely restricted water flow through the heat exchanger due to scale accumulation, a failed circulation pump (in recirculation-equipped models), or a blocked water path. It can also occur if the unit fires at full capacity with very low water flow.

Heat exchanger overheating is a serious condition that can lead to premature failure of this expensive component. Repeated overheating events will significantly shorten the heat exchanger lifespan.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: The heat exchanger is extremely hot when this error occurs. Do not touch any metal surfaces. Allow the unit to cool for at least 30 minutes before servicing.

causes.cause: Severe scale buildup in heat exchanger

fixSteps.title: Let Unit Cool
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit and allow it to cool for at least 30 minutes before any inspection.

fixSteps.title: Clean Inlet Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the cold water inlet filter to ensure full flow to the heat exchanger.

fixSteps.title: Descale Heat Exchanger
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a full descaling flush using isolation valves and a circulation pump with descaling solution for 60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check Recirculation Pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the unit has built-in recirculation, verify the pump is running when it should be. Listen for pump operation or check for voltage at the pump.

whenToCallPro: If descaling does not resolve the overheating, call a Navien technician to test the thermistor and recirculation pump.

faq.q: Can E016 damage the heat exchanger?
faq.a: Yes, repeated overheating can cause the heat exchanger to crack or fail prematurely.

faq.q: How much does a Navien heat exchanger cost?
faq.a: A replacement heat exchanger typically costs $500-$1,200 installed. Check if your unit is still under warranty.

title: Exhaust Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The exhaust temperature sensor is reading out of range or has failed.
fullDescription:
Error E030 indicates the exhaust temperature sensor (thermistor) is providing readings outside the expected range. This sensor monitors the temperature of exhaust gases leaving the heat exchanger to ensure safe and efficient combustion.

The sensor may have failed open, failed short, or its wiring may be damaged. Without accurate exhaust temperature readings, the control board cannot ensure safe operation and will shut the unit down.

In some cases, actual exhaust overtemperature due to combustion problems can trigger this error before the sensor itself has failed.
safetyWarning: Exhaust temperature monitoring is a critical safety function. Do not attempt to bypass this sensor. Abnormal exhaust temperatures may indicate combustion problems.

causes.cause: Loose or corroded sensor wiring connection
Actual exhaust overtemperature from combustion issue

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the exhaust temperature sensor connector is firmly seated on the control board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Sensor Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the sensor wires for damage, melting, or corrosion along their length.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read approximately 10k-15k ohms (check service manual for exact values).

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the resistance is out of range, replace the exhaust temperature sensor with a Navien OEM part.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not resolve the error, a technician should perform a combustion analysis to rule out exhaust overtemperature.

faq.q: Where is the exhaust temperature sensor?
faq.a: It is typically located on the exhaust pipe coming out of the top of the heat exchanger.

faq.q: Can I use a universal thermistor?
faq.a: No, use the OEM Navien sensor to ensure the correct resistance curve for your model.

title: Outlet Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The outlet water temperature sensor is reading out of range or has failed.
fullDescription:
Error E032 on Navien tankless water heaters indicates the outlet water temperature sensor (thermistor) has failed or is providing readings outside the acceptable range. This sensor is critical for regulating the output water temperature.

Without an accurate outlet temperature reading, the control board cannot modulate the gas valve to maintain the desired hot water temperature. The unit will shut down as a safety precaution to prevent delivering dangerously hot water.

This sensor typically fails due to age, exposure to very high temperatures, or a corroded connection.
safetyWarning: A failed outlet temperature sensor could allow water to overheat if the unit were to operate. Do not attempt to bypass this sensor.

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the outlet temperature sensor connector is firmly plugged into the control board.

fixSteps.title: Measure Sensor Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure its resistance with a multimeter. Compare the reading to the specification in the service manual.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the reading is out of range, replace the outlet temperature sensor with an OEM Navien part.

fixSteps.title: Verify Operation
fixSteps.instruction: After replacing the sensor, run hot water and verify the displayed outlet temperature matches the set temperature.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not clear the error, the control board may need replacement by a certified technician.

faq.q: Where is the outlet temperature sensor?
faq.a: It is located on the hot water outlet pipe, typically near the top of the heat exchanger.

faq.q: How do I know which sensor to order?
faq.a: Reference your model number (on the rating plate) when ordering. Different Navien models may use different sensors.

title: Inlet Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The inlet water temperature sensor is reading out of range or has failed.
fullDescription:
Error E038 indicates the cold water inlet temperature sensor (thermistor) is providing abnormal readings or has failed. The control board uses the inlet temperature reading along with the outlet temperature to calculate the required heat input and modulate the burner.

Without an accurate inlet temperature, the unit cannot properly calculate the temperature rise needed and will shut down. This sensor is exposed to incoming cold water and can fail due to corrosion, mineral deposits, or age.

This is generally a straightforward repair involving sensor replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the inlet temperature sensor is securely connected to the control board.

fixSteps.title: Measure Sensor Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure its resistance. Compare to the service manual specification for the current water temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If resistance is out of specification, replace with an OEM Navien inlet temperature sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the replacement sensor also triggers the error, the control board may be at fault.

faq.q: Will E038 damage my unit?
faq.a: No, the unit safely shuts down. However, you will not have hot water until it is resolved.

faq.q: Is the inlet sensor the same as the outlet sensor?
faq.a: They may be different part numbers. Always order the correct sensor for your model and location.

title: Condensate Drain Blockage
shortDescription: The condensate drain is blocked or the condensate trap is full.
fullDescription:
Error E046 indicates the Navien unit has detected a backup in the condensate drainage system. Condensing tankless water heaters produce acidic condensate during normal operation, and this must drain freely through the condensate trap and drain line.

When the condensate cannot drain, it can back up into the combustion chamber and cause corrosion, reduced efficiency, and eventual component failure. The condensate trap can become clogged with mineral deposits, algae, or debris.

Regular maintenance of the condensate drain system is important, especially in areas with hard water.

causes.cause: Frozen condensate line in cold weather

fixSteps.title: Locate Condensate Drain
fixSteps.instruction: Find the condensate drain line coming from the bottom of the unit. It is typically a small-diameter clear or white tube.

fixSteps.title: Clear the Drain Line
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drain line and blow through it or use a small brush to clear any blockage. Flush with clean water.

fixSteps.title: Clean the Condensate Trap
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the condensate trap. Flush it with warm water and vinegar to dissolve mineral deposits.

fixSteps.title: Reset and Test
fixSteps.instruction: Reinstall the drain line and trap, then restart the unit. Run hot water and verify condensate drains freely.

whenToCallPro: If the drain is clear but the error persists, the condensate level sensor may be faulty.

faq.q: Is condensate from a tankless water heater acidic?
faq.a: Yes, it is mildly acidic (pH 3-4). Most plumbing codes require it to be neutralized before entering a drain.

faq.q: How often should I clean the condensate trap?
faq.a: At least once a year as part of annual maintenance.

title: Condensate Drain Sensor Error
shortDescription: The condensate drain level sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription:
Error E047 indicates the condensate drain level sensor is not functioning properly. This sensor monitors the condensate water level to prevent overflow and backup into the combustion chamber.

Unlike E046 which indicates an actual blockage, E047 points to the sensor itself being faulty. The sensor may be stuck, corroded, or its wiring may be damaged.

This error can sometimes be caused by mineral buildup on the sensor probe, which can be cleaned without replacement.
symptoms: May appear even when condensate is draining properly

fixSteps.title: Clean the Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the condensate sensor and clean any mineral deposits from the probe with vinegar and a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the sensor wiring for corrosion, damage, or loose connections at the control board.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the sensor provides a signal change when submerged in water versus dry. Refer to the service manual for expected values.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, replace the condensate level sensor.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not clear the error, a technician should check the control board.

faq.q: Can I bypass the condensate sensor?
faq.a: No. The sensor is a safety device that prevents condensate overflow and potential water damage.

faq.q: How much does a condensate sensor cost?
faq.a: Typically $30-$60 for the OEM Navien part.

title: Abnormal Fan Speed
shortDescription: The combustion fan is not operating at the correct speed.
fullDescription:
Error E055 indicates the combustion fan (blower) is not achieving the commanded speed. The control board monitors fan RPM to ensure proper air-to-fuel ratio for safe and efficient combustion.

The fan may be running too slow due to bearing wear, debris accumulation, or an electrical issue. It could also be running too fast if there is a feedback signal problem. Proper fan speed is critical for maintaining the correct combustion air mixture.

This error can appear intermittently if the fan bearings are starting to wear or if there is an intermittent wiring connection.
safetyWarning: Incorrect combustion fan speed can lead to incomplete combustion and carbon monoxide production. Ensure CO detectors are functioning properly.

causes.cause: Debris or dust on fan blades

symptoms: Unusual humming or grinding noise from fan

fixSteps.title: Listen to the Fan
fixSteps.instruction: When the unit attempts to start, listen for unusual grinding, squealing, or vibration from the combustion fan.

fixSteps.title: Check Fan Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify all fan motor electrical connections are secure at both the motor and control board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Fan Blades
fixSteps.instruction: Visually inspect the fan blades for debris, dust accumulation, or damage that could cause imbalance.

fixSteps.title: Test Fan Motor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure voltage at the fan motor terminals when the unit calls for heat. Compare to the expected voltage in the service manual.

whenToCallPro: If the fan makes abnormal noise or the wiring checks out fine, a technician should test the motor and replace it if needed.

faq.q: How long do Navien fan motors last?
faq.a: Typically 10-15 years with normal use, though bearing wear can occur sooner in dusty environments.

faq.q: Can I oil the fan bearings?
faq.a: Most Navien fan motors have sealed bearings that cannot be serviced. Replacement is required when they fail.

title: Fan Motor Lock
shortDescription: The combustion fan motor is locked or not rotating.
fullDescription:
Error E109 indicates the combustion fan motor has completely failed to rotate. Unlike E055 (abnormal speed), E109 means the fan is completely locked or not responding to power signals at all.

This is typically caused by a seized motor, a failed motor winding, or a wiring failure. The unit cannot operate without the combustion fan as it provides the necessary air for safe combustion.

A locked fan motor will need to be replaced in most cases, as these motors are not typically serviceable.
safetyWarning: Do not attempt to manually force the fan to spin while the unit is powered. Disconnect power before any inspection.

fixSteps.title: Power Off Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit and disconnect power at the breaker or unplug the unit.

fixSteps.title: Check Fan Motor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the fan motor electrical connector is firmly seated.

fixSteps.title: Test for Free Rotation
fixSteps.instruction: With power disconnected, try to gently spin the fan blade by hand. If it does not rotate freely, the motor bearings are seized.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fan Motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is seized or does not respond, it must be replaced with an OEM Navien fan motor assembly.

whenToCallPro: This repair typically requires a professional as it involves removing and replacing the fan motor assembly and verifying combustion after installation.

faq.q: Can I repair a seized fan motor?
faq.a: No, Navien fan motors are not serviceable and must be replaced as a complete assembly.

faq.q: How much does a Navien fan motor cost?
faq.a: Typically $200-$400 for the part, plus $100-$200 for professional installation.

title: Exhaust Blockage Detected
shortDescription: The unit has detected a blockage in the exhaust venting system.
fullDescription:
Error E110 indicates the Navien unit has detected a significant restriction or blockage in the exhaust venting system. This error is triggered by the air pressure sensor detecting back-pressure that exceeds safe operating limits.

Common causes include ice formation at the vent termination in cold weather, bird nests, wasp nests, or other debris blocking the vent. Improper vent installation with too many elbows or excessive length can also cause this error.

This is a critical safety error as a blocked exhaust vent can cause carbon monoxide to enter the living space.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: A blocked exhaust vent can cause deadly carbon monoxide to enter your home. Ensure CO detectors are working. If CO alarm sounds, evacuate immediately and call 911.

causes.cause: Ice or snow blocking vent termination
Bird or insect nest in vent
Excessive vent length or too many elbows

symptoms: Unit shuts down and will not restart

fixSteps.title: Check Vent Termination Outside
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and inspect the vent termination for ice, snow, bird nests, wasp nests, or other obstructions. Clear any blockages.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Full Vent Run
fixSteps.instruction: Check the entire vent pipe from the unit to the termination for disconnections, sags, or crushed sections.

fixSteps.title: Verify Vent Screen
fixSteps.instruction: If the vent termination has a bird screen, make sure it is not clogged with debris or ice.

fixSteps.title: Reset and Test
fixSteps.instruction: After clearing the blockage, reset the unit and run hot water to verify normal operation.

whenToCallPro: If the vent appears clear but the error persists, a technician should check the air pressure sensor and perform a combustion analysis.

faq.q: Can I install a longer vent to prevent ice?
faq.a: Adding length may exceed the maximum. A wind-resistant or anti-freeze vent cap is a better solution.

faq.q: Why does this happen more in winter?
faq.a: Condensate in the exhaust can freeze at the vent termination in sub-freezing temperatures.

title: Flow Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water flow sensor is not reading correctly or has failed.
fullDescription:
Error E302 indicates the water flow sensor is providing abnormal readings or no signal at all. The flow sensor measures the rate of water flowing through the unit, which is essential for determining when to activate the burner and how much heat to apply.

A failed flow sensor will prevent the unit from detecting water demand and firing the burner. Common causes include mineral deposits on the turbine, a failed Hall effect sensor, or debris blocking the turbine.

This error can sometimes be resolved by cleaning the flow sensor, but replacement may be necessary if the sensor's electronics have failed.

causes.cause: Mineral deposits on flow sensor turbine
Failed Hall effect sensor in flow meter
Debris blocking the flow sensor turbine

symptoms: Unit does not activate when hot water is opened

fixSteps.title: Check Flow Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the flow sensor cable is securely connected to the control board.

fixSteps.title: Clean Flow Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the flow sensor and clean the turbine of any mineral deposits or debris. Soak in vinegar if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test Flow Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Reinstall the flow sensor and open a hot water faucet. Watch the control panel for flow rate display to verify the sensor is responding.

fixSteps.title: Replace Flow Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not restore function, replace the flow sensor with an OEM Navien part.

whenToCallPro: If the flow sensor replacement does not resolve the issue, a technician should check the control board.

faq.q: How does the flow sensor work?
faq.a: A small turbine spins as water flows through it. A Hall effect sensor counts the rotations to determine flow rate.

faq.q: How much does a Navien flow sensor cost?
faq.a: Typically $40-$80 for the OEM part.

title: Water Leak Detected
shortDescription: The internal leak detection sensor has detected water inside the unit.
fullDescription:
Error E351 indicates the water leak sensor inside the Navien unit has detected the presence of water where it should not be. This means there is an internal leak from a pipe connection, heat exchanger, or other water-carrying component.

Internal leaks in a tankless water heater can be caused by a cracked heat exchanger, loose fittings, failed seals or o-rings, or freeze damage. A leaking heat exchanger is the most serious scenario and may require significant repair or unit replacement.

This error should be addressed promptly to prevent water damage to the unit's electronic components and surrounding area.
safetyWarning: Turn off the water supply to the unit immediately to prevent water damage. If the unit is gas-powered, also turn off the gas supply.

fixSteps.title: Shut Off Water and Gas
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately close the water isolation valves and gas supply to the unit.

fixSteps.title: Remove Front Cover
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front panel to identify the source of the leak. Place towels or a bucket underneath.

fixSteps.title: Identify Leak Source
fixSteps.instruction: Slowly open the water supply and observe where water is leaking from. Check connections, fittings, and the heat exchanger.

fixSteps.title: Tighten or Repair
fixSteps.instruction: If the leak is from a connection, tighten it. If from a gasket, replace it. If from the heat exchanger, professional repair is needed.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is from the heat exchanger or you cannot identify the source, call a Navien-certified technician.

faq.q: Does Navien warranty cover heat exchanger leaks?
faq.a: Navien offers a 15-year heat exchanger warranty on most residential models when properly registered.

faq.q: Can freeze damage cause leaks?
faq.a: Yes. If the unit loses power during freezing weather, water inside can freeze and crack the heat exchanger or fittings.

title: Recirculation Pump Fault
shortDescription: The built-in recirculation pump has malfunctioned.
fullDescription:
Error E407 indicates the internal recirculation pump in Navien NPE series units has failed or is not operating correctly. The recirculation pump circulates hot water through the return line to provide instant hot water at fixtures.

The pump may have failed mechanically, have an electrical issue, or be air-locked. This error only applies to Navien models equipped with a built-in recirculation pump.

While the recirculation pump failure does not prevent the unit from producing hot water, it will result in longer wait times at fixtures and reduced convenience.

causes.cause: Air lock in the recirculation loop

symptoms: Long wait for hot water at fixtures
Hot water available but takes longer

fixSteps.title: Check Pump Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the recirculation pump electrical connection is secure.

fixSteps.title: Bleed Air from Pump
fixSteps.instruction: Open the bleed screw on the pump slightly to release trapped air. Close it once water flows out steadily.

fixSteps.title: Listen for Pump
fixSteps.instruction: With the unit on and recirculation active, listen for pump operation. A humming with no flow may indicate an air lock or seized impeller.

fixSteps.title: Replace Pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump has failed, it must be replaced with an OEM Navien recirculation pump.

whenToCallPro: If bleeding the pump and checking wiring does not resolve the error, the pump motor likely needs professional replacement.

faq.q: Can I still get hot water with E407?
faq.a: Yes, the main water heating function still works. You will just have to wait longer for hot water to reach fixtures.

faq.q: How much does a Navien recirculation pump cost?
faq.a: Typically $150-$300 for the pump assembly.

title: Recirculation Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The recirculation return line temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription:
Error E421 indicates the temperature sensor on the recirculation return line is reading out of range or has failed. This sensor monitors the temperature of water returning from the recirculation loop to determine when the pump should activate.

Without a functioning return temperature sensor, the system cannot intelligently control the recirculation pump based on return water temperature. The sensor may have failed due to age, corrosion, or a wiring issue.

This error only affects the recirculation function and does not prevent normal hot water production on demand.
symptoms: Recirculation pump runs continuously or not at all

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the recirculation temperature sensor connector is securely plugged into the control board.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. Compare to the specification in the service manual.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the resistance is out of specification, replace the recirculation temperature sensor.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not clear the error, a technician should check the control board recirculation circuit.

faq.q: Can I disable recirculation and use the unit normally?
faq.a: Yes, turning off recirculation will allow normal on-demand hot water, though you will lose the instant hot water feature.

faq.q: Where is this sensor located?
faq.a: On the recirculation return line connection at the bottom of the unit.

title: Communication Error - Cascading System
shortDescription: Communication failure between cascaded Navien units.
fullDescription:
Error E434 indicates a communication failure between multiple Navien tankless water heaters that are cascaded (linked together) to provide higher hot water capacity. The units communicate over a data cable to coordinate firing and load sharing.

This error typically occurs when the communication cable between units is damaged, disconnected, or improperly terminated. It can also occur if one unit in the cascade system loses power.

In a cascaded system, all units must be able to communicate to properly distribute the hot water load. When communication fails, the units may not fire in the correct sequence.

causes.cause: One unit in cascade has lost power
Failed communication circuit on control board

symptoms: E434 displayed on one or more units

fixSteps.title: Check Communication Cables
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the communication cables between all cascaded units are securely connected at both ends.

fixSteps.title: Verify Power to All Units
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm all units in the cascade system have power and are turned on.

fixSteps.title: Check DIP Switch Settings
fixSteps.instruction: Verify each unit has a unique cascade address set on its DIP switches according to the installation manual.

fixSteps.title: Replace Communication Cable
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable appears damaged, replace it with a new communication cable of the proper specification.

whenToCallPro: If all cables are connected and DIP switches are correctly set but the error persists, a technician should diagnose the communication circuit on the control boards.

faq.q: Can each unit still work independently?
faq.a: Individual units may still produce hot water, but they will not coordinate, which can lead to temperature fluctuations.

faq.q: How many Navien units can be cascaded?
faq.a: Up to 16 units can be cascaded together depending on the model series.

title: Abnormal PCB / Control Board Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription:
Error E480 indicates the main PCB (printed circuit board) or control board has detected an internal error that prevents normal operation. This is a general control board fault that can encompass various internal diagnostic failures.

The control board is the brain of the Navien unit, managing all sensors, the gas valve, fan, and ignition system. An internal fault may be caused by a power surge, moisture intrusion, component aging, or a manufacturing defect.

This error typically requires control board replacement, which is a significant repair. If the unit is under warranty, the control board may be covered.

causes.cause: Power surge damage to control board
Moisture or condensate intrusion on board

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit, disconnect power for 60 seconds, then reconnect and restart. Some transient PCB errors can clear with a power cycle.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for Moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and check the control board for signs of moisture, condensation, or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Check All Connections
fixSteps.instruction: Verify all wire harness connections to the control board are secure and free of corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace Control Board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the control board must be replaced by a certified technician.

whenToCallPro: If a power cycle does not clear E480, a Navien-certified technician is needed for control board diagnosis and replacement.

faq.q: How much does a Navien control board cost?
faq.a: Control boards typically cost $200-$500 for the part, depending on the model.

faq.q: Is the control board covered under warranty?
faq.a: Navien typically offers a 5-year parts warranty. Check your registration and model for specific coverage.

faq.q: Should I install a surge protector?
faq.a: Yes, a surge protector is recommended for all tankless water heaters to protect the control board from power surges.
